usage_sections: Features: - Comes with a built-in console LDAP client - All parameters can be conrolled via a conveinent URL (see below) - Supports integrated windows authentication (SSPI) both with NTLM and with KERBEROS - Supports channel binding (for ntlm and kerberos not SSPI) - Supports encryption (f
Installation: Via GIT python3 setup.py install OR pip install msldap

Usage: Please note that this is a library, and was not intended to be used as a command line program. Whit this noted, the projects packs a fully functional LDAP interactive client. When installing the msldap module with setup.py install a new binary will appear called msldap (shocking

arguments.name: -nt
arguments.description: kerberos-password (dc option param must be used)

arguments.name: --target
arguments.description: Target host, IP, MAC, or URL the tool operates on.

arguments.name: --output
arguments.description: Output file path for the report or capture.

arguments.name: --version
arguments.description: Print the tool version and exit.

arguments.name: --wordlist
arguments.description: Path to a wordlist (users, passwords, paths).

arguments.name: --dry-run
arguments.description: Print what would be done without making any changes.

arguments.name: --force
arguments.description: Force the action even if a safety check would block it.

chain_examples.note: Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with skelsec/minikerberos (score=11); pair in the chain planner.

Phase 3: same attack_surface=['web', 'ad'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

documentation.how_to_use: Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `OPTIONAL. Specifies the root tree of all queries`.
